maitha.root: م-ي-ث — from m-y-th, soft, supple; Maytha is a sandy-soft plain. Maitha names a girl gentle as fertile lowland.
maitha.iuh: Meem of love opens the way, Ya stretches toward the far, Tha bears a soft witness, Alif resolves at the origin. Love, reaching, gentle witness, return to the One.

majid.root: م-ج-د — from m-j-d, to be glorious, noble. Al-Majīd is one of the divine names: the All-Glorious. Sūrat al-Burūj 85:15 names Him 'Dhū'l-ʿArsh al-Majīd'.
majid.iuh: Meem of love opens, Alif anchors the One, Jeem of inner beauty turns the heart, Dal becomes the door of return. Love, origin, beauty, return — a glory that draws others toward God.

bearers.name: Majid Majidi
bearers.desc: Iranian filmmaker, director of 'Children of Heaven' (1997, Oscar-nominated)

malaika.root: م-ل-ك — plural of malak, angel. The Quran uses malāʾika for the order of luminous beings made of light (Q 2:30, 35:1).
malaika.iuh: Meem of love begins, Lam of authority holds, Alif of origin stands, Ya reaches upward, Kaf of creation forms, Ta of completion seals — the plural form of light-bearing servants of God.

malak.root: م-ل-ك — from m-l-k, to possess, to reign. Malak is the angel, the luminous messenger whose form is fixed by God; same root as malik (king) and mulk (dominion).
malak.iuh: Meem of love opens, Lam of authority stands, Alif anchors the One, Kaf of creation forms. Love, authority, origin, kun — the messenger whose existence is pure obedience.

maleeka.root: م-ل-ك — from m-l-k, to possess, to reign. Malīka is the queen, feminine of malik; the woman who holds authority with grace.
maleeka.iuh: Meem of love begins, Lam of authority holds, Ya reaches in yearning, Kaf of creation, Ta of completion. A reign formed in love and finished in grace.

malika.root: م-ل-ك — from m-l-k, to own and to rule. Malika is queen, the feminine of malik; Bilqīs, queen of Sabaʾ, is named al-malika in Sūrat al-Naml.
malika.iuh: Meem opens with love, Lam stands in authority, Kaf is the kun of creation, the final Ta seals the form. Love, rule, creativity, manifestation — queenship that protects.

bearers.name: Malika Oufkir
bearers.desc: Moroccan author of 'Stolen Lives', held 20 years in desert prison by Hassan II

mamadou.root: م-ح-م-د — Wolof and West African Mande compression of Muḥammad, through the consonants m-m-d. Same root: ḥ-m-d, to praise.
mamadou.iuh: Meem of love opens, the inner letters echo the Prophet's name, and the final Dal closes the door of return. A West African voice for the Praised One.

bearers.name: Mamadou Sakho
bearers.desc: French international football defender, Liverpool and PSG

bearers.name: Mamadou Tandja
bearers.desc: President of Niger 1999–2010

mamdouh.root: م-د-ح — from m-d-ḥ, to praise. Mamdūḥ is the passive participle: the one who is praised, celebrated for real merit.
mamdouh.iuh: Meem of love opens, Meem doubles the love within, Dal of return, Waw of connection, Ha of presence — a praise that is lived, not announced.

manahil.root: م-ن-ه-ل — from n-h-l, to drink at a spring. Manāhil is the plural of manhal, the spring or watering-place at which travellers refresh themselves.
manahil.iuh: Meem of love opens, Nun of depth, Alif of origin, Ha of presence, Lam of authority — many springs, freely given.

manal.root: م-ن-ل — from n-y-l / n-w-l, to attain, to reach. Manāl is what is attainable, the gift within reach, the achievement.
manal.iuh: Meem of love opens, Nun of depth, Alif of origin, Lam of authority. Love and depth and origin meet in attainment.

manar.root: م-ن-ر — from n-w-r, light. Manār is the lighthouse, the beacon, the place where light is shown; same root as nūr.
manar.iuh: Meem of love begins, Nun of depth, Alif of origin, Ra of mercy-in-motion — a beacon raised over deep waters.

mansour.root: ن-ص-ر — from n-ṣ-r, to help, to grant victory. Manṣūr is the passive participle: the one rendered victorious by divine help.
mansour.iuh: Meem of love opens, Nun of depth, Sad of sincerity, Waw of connection, Ra of mercy-in-motion. Victory granted, never seized.

bearers.name: Abu Ja'far al-Mansur
bearers.desc: Second Abbasid caliph, founder of Baghdad in 762 CE

bearers.name: Mansour Bahrami
bearers.desc: Iranian-French tennis player and entertainer

mansur.root: ن-ص-ر — from n-ṣ-r, to render victorious. Manṣūr names the one given help by God, the one whose victory is not his own work but a gift.
mansur.iuh: Meem of love, Nun of depth, Sad of sincerity, Waw of connection, Ra of mercy-in-motion. Help received, victory conferred.

bearers.name: Mansur al-Hallaj
bearers.desc: Persian Sufi mystic executed in Baghdad for the utterance 'ana al-Haqq'

bearers.name: Abu Ja'far al-Mansur
bearers.desc: Second Abbasid caliph, founder of Baghdad

maram.root: ر-و-م — from r-w-m, to seek, to aim at. Marām is what one strives toward, the wished-for object, the aspiration.
maram.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ra of mercy-in-motion, Alif of origin, Meem closes again in love. The wish itself is bracketed in love.

mardhiyah.root: ر-ض-ي — from r-ḍ-y, to be pleased, to find favor. Marḍiyya is the passive participle: she who is found pleasing — the term used for the soul addressed in Surat al-Fajr 89:28 (yā ayyatuhā al-nafs al-muṭmaʾinnah... raḍiyatan marḍiyyah).
mardhiyah.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ra of mercy-in-motion, Dad of strength, Ya reaching, Ta of completion. A soul pleasing because it first received divine grace.

mariama.root: Non-Arabic origin — West African Fula and Mande form of Maryam. The Quranic Maryam is the only woman named in the Quran, mother of Sayyiduna ʿĪsā (AS).
mariama.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ra of mercy, Ya of reaching, Alif of origin, Meem closes in love, Alif closes the form. A name framed by love at both ends.

bearers.name: Mariama Ba
bearers.desc: Senegalese author of 'So Long a Letter' (Une si longue lettre, 1979)

mariem.root: Non-Arabic origin — North African and French-speaking spelling of Maryam, the mother of Sayyiduna ʿĪsā (AS), the only woman named in the Quran.
mariem.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ra of mercy-in-motion, Ya of reaching, Meem closes in love. A name bracketed by love and lit by mercy.

mariyah.root: Old Arabic — Māriya means white, radiant, bright. The Coptic Māriya bint Shamʿūn, gift from al-Muqawqis ruler of Egypt to the Prophet ﷺ, was mother of the Prophet's son Ibrahim.
mariyah.iuh: Meem of love opens, Alif of origin, Ra of mercy, Ya of reaching, Ta of completion. A radiance held within love and finished in form.

bearers.name: Mariyah al-Qibtiyya
bearers.desc: Coptic concubine of the Prophet ﷺ, mother of his son Ibrahim

mariyam.root: Non-Arabic origin — Quranic Maryam, mother of ʿĪsā (AS); the only woman named in the Quran. From Hebrew Miriam, sister of Mūsā and Hārūn (AS).
mariyam.iuh: Meem of love, Ra of mercy, Ya of reaching, Alif of origin, Meem closes in love. Bracketed in love, shot through with mercy.

marouane.root: Old Arabic — Marwān is a pre-Islamic Arabic name meaning silex stone, flint. North African/French spelling preserves the French nasal -ouane for Arabic -wān.
marouane.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ra of mercy-in-motion, Waw of connection, Alif of origin, Nun of depth. Mercy and connection meet at the origin.

bearers.name: Marouane Fellaini
bearers.desc: Belgian-Moroccan international footballer, Manchester United midfielder

marwa.root: م-ر-و — from the root naming the smooth white stones. Al-Marwa is the hill in Mecca paired with al-Safa; together they form the saʿy of Hajj and ʿUmra, retracing Hajar's search for water for her infant Ismaʿil. Q 2:158: 'inna al-ṣafā wa-l-marwa min shaʿāʾir Allāh'.
marwa.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ra of mercy-in-motion, Waw of connection, Ta of completion. The saʿy itself: love, mercy, connection, return.

marwan.root: Old Arabic — Marwān, from the consonant cluster naming silex stone, flint, the white hard rock of the Hijaz. Pre-Islamic Arab name preserved through Umayyad caliphal use.
marwan.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ra of mercy, Waw of connection, Alif of origin, Nun of depth. A hard kindness, an old strength.

bearers.name: Marwan I ibn al-Hakam
bearers.desc: Fourth Umayyad caliph (684–685), founder of the Marwanid line

bearers.name: Marwan al-Bashshara
bearers.desc: Last Umayyad caliph Marwan II ibn Muhammad, ruled 744–750 CE

mateen.root: م-ت-ن — from m-t-n, to be firm, sturdy. Al-Matīn is one of the divine names: the Firm, the Steadfast in strength (Q 51:58 'innallāha huwa ar-razzāq dhū al-quwwati al-matīn').
mateen.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ta-light bears witness, Ya reaches in yearning, Nun of depth seals. A strength that is also gentle, a firmness with mercy in it.

mawaddah.root: و-د-د — from w-d-d, to love with affection. Mawadda is the deep abiding love between spouses, mentioned in Q 30:21: 'jaʿala baynakum mawaddatan wa-raḥmah' — He placed between you love and mercy.
mawaddah.iuh: Meem of love opens, Waw of connection, Dal of return, Ta of completion. Love made into a connecting door, sealed in form.

maya.root: Multiple origins — In Arabic māʾ refers to water (m-w-h). In Persian and Hindi-Urdu maya carries the sense of grace, illusion, charm. Used across many cultures for its gentle openness.
maya.iuh: Meem of love opens, Alif of origin, Ya of reaching, Alif of return. Love at the origin, reaching outward, returning to origin again — a name shaped like a circle.

mayar.root: م-ي-ر — from m-y-r, to provide provisions. Mayār is the supply of food, the act of provisioning; also used for standard, measure. Trending Gulf girls' name.
mayar.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ya of reaching, Alif of origin, Ra of mercy-in-motion. Love that reaches, anchored in the One, made into mercy.

maymouna.root: ي-م-ن — from y-m-n, right side, blessing, good fortune. Maymūna means blessed, auspicious; same root as yamīn (right hand) and yumn (good fortune).
maymouna.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ya of reaching, Meem doubles the love, Waw of connection, Nun of depth, Ta of completion. A doubled love made stable, deep, finished.

bearers.name: Maymunah bint al-Harith
bearers.desc: Last wife of the Prophet ﷺ and Mother of the Believers

maymuna.root: ي-م-ن — from y-m-n, right side, blessing. Maymūna is blessed, fortunate, auspicious — the woman who carries the right-side blessing.
maymuna.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ya of reaching, Meem of love again, Waw of connection, Nun of depth, Alif of return. Doubled love, connected and deep.

bearers.name: Maymunah bint al-Harith
bearers.desc: Wife of the Prophet ﷺ and Mother of the Believers, sister-in-law of al-ʿAbbās

maymunah.root: ي-م-ن — from y-m-n, right side, blessing, good fortune. Maymūna bint al-Ḥārith was the last wife of the Prophet ﷺ taken in marriage.
maymunah.iuh: Meem of love, Ya of reaching, Meem of love, Waw of connection, Nun of depth, Ta of completion. Doubled love, deep, sealed.

bearers.name: Maymunah bint al-Harith
bearers.desc: Wife of the Prophet ﷺ and Mother of the Believers

maysa.root: م-ي-س — from m-y-s, to walk with a swaying graceful gait. Maysāʾ is the woman whose walk has poise, an Arabic image of dignity in motion.
maysa.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ya of reaching, Sin of secret, Alif of origin. Love reaches, reveals a secret of grace, returns to the One.

medhi.root: ه-د-ي — Persian/French spelling of Mahdī, from h-d-y, to guide. Al-Mahdī, the Rightly-Guided One, is the awaited figure of the end times in Islamic eschatology.
medhi.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ha of presence, Dal of return, Ya of reaching. Love made into presence, then the door of return, then the reaching toward what is awaited.

meerab.root: م-ي-ر / Persian compound — South Asian compound from mīr (chief, master) and āb (water): chief of water, master of distribution. A name popular in Pakistan after the 2022 drama Khuda Aur Mohabbat.
meerab.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ya of reaching, Ra of mercy-in-motion, Alif of origin, Ba of foundation. Love that reaches, becomes mercy, founds a household.

mehak.root: Persian/Urdu origin — mahak (مہک) means fragrance, scent. A name common in Pakistan for girls, evoking soft attraction.
mehak.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ha of presence, Kaf of creation. Love made present, formed into being — a quiet creating fragrance.

mehdi.root: ه-د-ي — Persian/Arabic Mahdī, from h-d-y, to guide. Al-Mahdī, 'the rightly-guided', is the awaited end-times figure in Islamic eschatology; a 5-star Iranian boys' name.
mehdi.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ha of presence, Dal of return, Ya of reaching. Presence opens the door of return so the reaching may begin.

meher.root: Persian origin — meher (مہر) carries layered meanings: kindness, love, the sun, the dowry given in marriage. From Avestan mithra, an old Iranian root.
meher.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ha of presence, Ra of mercy-in-motion. Love becomes presence, presence flows out as mercy.

mehernaz.root: Persian compound — meher (kindness, sun) + nāz (grace, pride). 'Pride of kindness' or 'graceful sun'.
mehernaz.iuh: Meem of love, Ha of presence, Ra of mercy, Nun of depth, Alif of origin, Zayn of subtle radiance. Kindness made luminous and proud.

meherunissa.root: Compound — Persian mihr (kindness, sun) + Arabic al-nisāʾ (the women). 'Sun of women' or 'kindness of women'; the original name of Mughal Empress Nur Jahan.
meherunissa.iuh: Meem of love opens, the inner letters trace kindness flowing into a place of feminine plurality. A title that became a name.

bearers.name: Nur Jahan (Mehrunnisa)
bearers.desc: Mughal Empress and twentieth wife of Emperor Jahangir, de facto ruler of Mughal India in his last years

mehmed.root: ح-م-د — Turkish/Ottoman form of Muḥammad, from ḥ-m-d, to praise. The Praised One; same root as Aḥmad, Maḥmūd, Ḥāmid.
mehmed.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ha-heavy of breath, Meem of love again, Dal of return. The Prophet's name in Ottoman vowels.

bearers.name: Mehmed II (the Conqueror)
bearers.desc: Ottoman Sultan who conquered Constantinople in 1453

mehmet.root: ح-م-د — Turkish form of Muḥammad, from ḥ-m-d, to praise. The Praised One, Turkey's most common male name.
mehmet.iuh: Meem opens with love, Ha breathes life, Meem closes in love, Ta seals as witness. The Prophet's name with Turkish closure.

bearers.name: Mehmet Akif Ersoy
bearers.desc: Turkish poet, author of the Turkish national anthem 'İstiklâl Marşı'

mehnaz.root: Persian compound — māh (moon) + nāz (pride, grace). 'Pride of the moon' or 'graceful as moonlight'.
mehnaz.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ha of presence, Nun of depth, Alif of origin, Zayn of subtle radiance. Moonlight made into pride.

mehreen.root: Persian origin — feminine form drawing on mihr (kindness, sun, affection). A soft name evoking the warmth of the sun and the warmth of love.
mehreen.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ha of presence, Ra of mercy, Ya of reaching, Nun of depth. Kindness made deep through reaching.

mehwish.root: Persian origin — māh-wash, 'moon-like', 'beautiful as the moon'. From Persian māh (moon) + wash (like).
mehwish.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ha of presence, Waw of connection, Sheen of radiance. A moonlike radiance carried through connection.

melek.root: م-ل-ك — Turkish form of malak, angel; same root as Arabic m-l-k (to own, to reign). The luminous messenger, the angel.
melek.iuh: Meem of love opens, Lam of authority, Kaf of creation. A formed authority, a created presence — the angel in three letters.

melia.root: Multiple origins — In Arabic milā can mean fullness, abundance. In Hawaiian melia is the plumeria flower. Used across cultures for its soft sound.
melia.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ya of reaching, Lam of authority, Ya of reaching again, Ta of completion. A reaching twice over.

melisa.root: Multiple origins — Turkish/Greek melisa from Greek mélissa (honey-bee). A Turkish girls' name with the gentleness of the bee's labor.
melisa.iuh: Meem of love opens, Lam of authority, Ya of reaching, Sin of secret, Ta of completion. A small reaching that yields sweetness.

memoona.root: ي-م-ن — South Asian/Urdu spelling of Maymūna; from y-m-n, right side, blessing. The blessed, the auspicious.
memoona.iuh: Meem of love opens, the Mim-Waw-Nun core repeats the blessing-name through Urdu vowels. Blessed twice — by sound and by intent.

bearers.name: Maymunah bint al-Harith
bearers.desc: Wife of the Prophet ﷺ and Mother of the Believers

meral.root: Turkish origin — meral (sometimes maral) is the red deer or gazelle, a poetic image of swift grace. Persian and Turkic both share the word.
meral.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ya of reaching, Ra of mercy-in-motion, Alif of origin, Lam of authority. A swift mercy with authority in it.

meriam.root: Non-Arabic origin — North African and French spelling of Maryam, mother of Sayyiduna ʿĪsā (AS).
meriam.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ra of mercy, Ya of reaching, Alif of origin, Meem closes in love. Mercy at the heart of love.

meriem.root: Non-Arabic origin — Algerian and North African spelling of Maryam, mother of Sayyiduna ʿĪsā (AS) — the only woman named in the Quran.
meriem.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ra of mercy-in-motion, Ya of reaching, Meem closes in love. A name bracketed in love, threaded with mercy.

mert.root: Persian/Turkish origin — mert means brave, manly, gallant, honourable. From the Persian mard (man, brave one).
mert.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ra of mercy-in-motion, Ta of completion. Mercy moving through love to completion — a complete bravery.

merve.root: م-ر-و — Turkish form of Marwa, the sacred hill of Mecca paired with al-Safa. The name carries the saʿy of Hajj as a daily reminder.
merve.iuh: Meem of love opens, Ra of mercy, Waw of connection, Ta of completion. The saʿy in four letters: love, mercy, joining, return.
